engineGuide.h2: Jaguar X Type Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Jaguar X Type across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The entry-level diesel for the X Type, a Ford-sourced Duratorq unit that offered economy but gained a reputation for EGR cooler fragility.
commonFailures: EGR valve and cooler clogging (60,000-80,000 miles)
Injector seal leakage leading to oil dilution
entries.cta: Get quotes for DLD-416 Jaguar X Type engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The most searched X Type diesel code in the UK, this updated 2.0D brought 163hp and Euro 4 compliance, but injector seal failures became its defining flaw.
compatibleVariants: 2.0D SE, 2.0D Sport, 2.0D Sport Premium, 2.0D Classic, 2.0D Estate
commonFailures: Injector seal failure causing oil dilution (40,000-70,000 miles)
DPF clogging on short journeys (post-2006 models)
entries.cta: Get quotes for DLD-420 Jaguar X Type engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Diesel (early)
entries.history: The larger diesel option introduced in late 2004, offering stronger torque for the X Type Estate, but prone to swirl flap failures in the inlet manifold.
commonFailures: Swirl flap failure in inlet manifold (50,000-80,000 miles)
entries.cta: Get quotes for 214D2 Jaguar X Type engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Diesel (late)
entries.history: The final evolution of the X Type diesel, with 155hp and improved emissions hardware, representing the most refined version of the Ford-sourced Duratorq.
entries.cta: Get quotes for DLD-422 Jaguar X Type engine replacement

entries.title: 2.1L Petrol V6
entries.history: Strictly a 2.1-litre unit despite its 2.0 badge, this front-wheel-drive entry petrol offered Jaguar refinement on a budget, but coil pack failures are common.
compatibleVariants: 2.0 V6, 2.0 V6 Classic, 2.0 V6 SE, 2.0 V6 Sport
commonFailures: Coil pack failure (one per cylinder, common failure)
Timing chain tensioner wear (cold start rattle)
Oil leaks from rocker cover gaskets
entries.cta: Get quotes for YB AJ-V6 Jaguar X Type engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol V6
entries.history: The mainstream petrol X Type and the most searched petrol variant in the UK, this 2.5 V6 powered both front and all-wheel drive versions with 196hp.
compatibleVariants: 2.5 V6, 2.5 V6 SE, 2.5 V6 Sport, 2.5 V6 Sport Premium, 2.5 V6 AWD, 2.5 V6 Executive
commonFailures: Oil leaks from rocker cover gaskets
entries.cta: Get quotes for XB AJ-V6 Jaguar X Type engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol V6
entries.history: The flagship performance petrol for the X Type, producing 230hp and shared with the Ford Mondeo ST220, offering genuine pace but with cooling system vulnerabilities.
compatibleVariants: 3.0 V6, 3.0 V6 SE, 3.0 V6 Sport, 3.0 V6 Sovereign, 3.0 V6 AWD, 3.0 V6 Executive
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ30 Jaguar X Type engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Jaguar X Type Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.group: Jaguar X Type Diesel Injector Seal Failure
problems.h4: Jaguar X Type Diesel Injector Seal Failure - The Oil Dilution Risk That Destroys Engines
problems.affectedModels: Jaguar X Type 2.0D (2005-2009, DLD-420 engine code), 2.2D (2005-2009, DLD-422/214D3)
problems.rootCause: The copper injector seals degrade over time, allowing combustion gases to blow past and contaminate the engine oil with diesel fuel. This oil dilution reduces lubrication properties, leading to accelerated bearing wear and eventual engine seizure if not addressed promptly. The DLD-420 engine is particularly susceptible.

repairOptions.tier: Injector Seal Replacement - Replace affected injector seals
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removal of injectors, new copper seals, injector leak-off test, oil and filter change
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early before oil dilution has caused bearing damage. Typically lasts 40,000-60,000 miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine replacement with upgraded injector seals, oil pump, timing components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only solution if engine has seized or bearings are damaged from prolonged oil dilution

problems.recommendation: If you catch injector seal failure early (oil smells of diesel, slight oil level rise), the seal replacement is absolutely the right call - it costs under £500 at a specialist and saves the engine. However, if you've been driving with the issue for months or the engine has started knocking, the oil dilution has already damaged the bottom end. On an X Type worth £1,500-£4,000, a dealer-priced engine replacement makes no financial sense, but a specialist-fitted rebuilt unit at £1,800-£3,200 keeps the car on the road for less than replacement cost.
problems.cta: Compare DLD-420 rebuilt engine prices for your Jaguar X Type

problems.group: Jaguar X Type AJ-V6 Timing Chain Tensioner Failure
problems.h4: Jaguar X Type AJ-V6 Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - The Cold Start Rattle That Kills Engines
problems.rootCause: The original plastic timing chain tensioners lose hydraulic pressure when the engine is off, allowing the chain to go slack. On cold start, the chain rattles against the guides until oil pressure builds. If ignored, the tensioner can fail completely, causing the chain to skip teeth or break, resulting in piston-to-valve contact and catastrophic engine damage.

repairOptions.tier: Preventative Timing Chain Replacement - Replace tensioners, guides and chain
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removal of timing covers, replacement of primary and secondary chains, tensioners, guides, and water pump while accessible
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended before failure occurs - at first signs of cold start rattle. Upgraded metal-cage tensioners last 80,000-100,000 miles

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / reconditioned engine after failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine replacement after chain failure has caused valve or piston damage
repairOptions.longevity: Only solution if engine has jumped timing and internal damage has occurred

problems.recommendation: The 2.5 V6 and 3.0 V6 are fantastic engines when maintained, but the timing chain tensioner is their Achilles' heel. If you hear a rattling noise for 1-2 seconds on cold start only, book a preventative chain replacement immediately - the specialist price of £1,200-£2,000 is well worth it for an enthusiast-owned X Type. However, if the rattle continues for longer or you've heard a loud bang followed by a non-starting engine, internal damage has occurred and a rebuilt engine is your only path forward. Given the car's value, the specialist-fit rebuilt route at £2,200-£3,800 is the maximum you should consider.
problems.cta: Compare AJ-V6 rebuilt engine prices for your Jaguar X Type

problems.group: Jaguar X Type Diesel EGR Cooler & Swirl Flap Failure
problems.h4: Jaguar X Type Diesel EGR Cooler & Swirl Flap Failure - The Power Loss Nightmare
problems.rootCause: The EGR cooler becomes clogged with soot, reducing exhaust gas recirculation efficiency and triggering limp mode. On 2.2D variants, the plastic swirl flaps in the inlet manifold break apart, with fragments potentially entering the combustion chamber. Both conditions cause power loss, poor throttle response, and can lead to DPF blockages.

repairOptions.tier: EGR Cooler / Inlet Manifold Replacement - Replace failed components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removal and replacement of EGR cooler, cleaning of intake system, new inlet manifold if swirl flaps have failed
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early and no debris has entered engine. New components last 50,000-70,000 miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine replacement including updated inlet manifold with metal swirl flaps, new EGR system
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary if swirl flap fragments have been ingested into cylinders or DPF is irreversibly blocked

problems.recommendation: If you're experiencing intermittent power loss and the check engine light, get the EGR system inspected immediately. A specialist EGR cooler replacement at £400-£800 is the economical fix. However, if swirl flap failure has occurred and debris has entered the engine, do not attempt a partial repair - the risk of future engine damage is too high. On an X Type 2.2D, the specialist rebuilt engine route at £1,800-£3,200 is the safe, cost-effective solution that includes upgraded components designed to prevent recurrence.
problems.cta: Compare DLD-420 and DLD-422 rebuilt engine prices for your Jaguar X Type

problems.group: Jaguar X Type AJ-V6 Coolant Crossover Pipe Failure
problems.h4: Jaguar X Type AJ-V6 Coolant Crossover Pipe Failure - The Overheating Catastrophe
problems.affectedModels: Jaguar X Type 2.5 V6 (2001-2007), 3.0 V6 (2001-2008), all AJ-V6 petrol variants
problems.rootCause: The plastic coolant crossover pipe (thermostat housing and pipe assembly) becomes brittle with age and heat cycling. It cracks at the join between the two cylinder banks, causing sudden coolant loss. The X Type's temperature gauge is notoriously unresponsive to rapid coolant loss, and drivers often don't realise there's a problem until the engine overheats and the heads warp.

repairOptions.tier: Coolant Crossover Pipe Replacement - Replace failed plastic components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drain cooling system, replace crossover pipe and thermostat housing, pressure test, refill and bleed
repairOptions.longevity: Effective prevention if replaced proactively. Aftermarket metal pipe upgrades available and recommended

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / reconditioned engine after overheating
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine replacement after head gasket failure or warped heads from overheating
repairOptions.longevity: Only solution if overheating has occurred and heads are warped or gaskets have failed

problems.recommendation: This is a classic case where prevention is dramatically cheaper than the cure. If you own a 2.5 or 3.0 V6 X Type with over 70,000 miles, have the plastic crossover pipe replaced proactively with an upgraded metal version - it's £250-£450 at a specialist and could save your engine. If you've already overheated the engine (steam from bonnet, loss of power, mayonnaise under oil cap), the damage is likely done. A specialist-fit rebuilt engine at £2,000-£3,500 is the economic ceiling for an X Type - anything above that, and it's time to consider breaking the car for parts.
problems.cta: Compare AJ-V6 rebuilt engine prices for your Jaguar X Type

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Jaguar X Type
finalCta.paragraph: The X Type's Ford-sourced engines have their weak points - diesel injector seals, V6 timing chain tensioners, EGR coolers, and plastic coolant pipes - but none of these failures have to be the end of the car. With specialist pricing often 40-60% below main dealer quotes and rebuilt engines carrying a 12-month warranty, your X Type can live on without breaking the bank. Enter your reg, compare prices from vetted UK Jaguar specialists, and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Jaguar X Type Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EnginesMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Jaguar X Type and engine code. Every engine supplied through EnginesMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Jaguar X Type Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every factory displacement fitted to the Jaguar X Type is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify your exact unit.

groups.title: Jaguar X Type Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Jaguar X Type 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre Duratorq diesel was the entry-level oil-burner for the X Type, arriving in 2003 as a 128hp unit before being upgraded to 163hp in 2005 with Euro 4 emissions compliance. This Ford-sourced engine became the most popular choice for fleet buyers and now dominates replacement search volume due to well-documented injector seal and EGR cooler failures.
items.cta: Get quotes for Jaguar X Type 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Jaguar X Type 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: Introduced in late 2004 as a higher-torque alternative for the X Type Estate and Sport models, the 2.2-litre Duratorq offered 145hp initially and later 155hp after the 2007 facelift. This unit shares the same injector seal vulnerabilities as the 2.0-litre but adds swirl flap failures in the plastic inlet manifold and turbo actuator problems at higher mileages.
items.cta: Get quotes for Jaguar X Type 2.2L diesel engines

groups.title: Jaguar X Type Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Jaguar X Type 2.1L Petrol Engines
items.description: Strictly a 2.1-litre V6 despite its "2.0" badging, this entry-level petrol engine was available only on front-wheel-drive X Type models from 2002 to 2005. It produced 156hp and offered Jaguar refinement at a lower price point, but coil pack failures are exceptionally common due to the individual coil-on-plug design.
items.cta: Compare Jaguar X Type 2.1L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jaguar X Type 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The most searched petrol variant in the X Type range, this 2.5-litre AJ-V6 powered both front and all-wheel-drive models with 196hp from 2001 to 2007. Enthusiasts appreciate its smooth power delivery, but the plastic timing chain tensioners are a well-documented weak point, causing cold start rattle and eventual catastrophic failure if ignored.
items.cta: Compare Jaguar X Type 2.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jaguar X Type 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The flagship performance petrol for the X Type, this 3.0-litre AJ-V6 produced 227-231hp and was shared with the Ford Mondeo ST220. Available only with all-wheel drive in the X Type, it delivered genuine pace but suffered from the same timing chain tensioner weaknesses as the 2.5-litre, plus additional cooling system vulnerabilities around the plastic thermostat housing and crossover pipes.
items.cta: Compare Jaguar X Type 3.0L petrol engine prices

2.5 V6 and 3.0 V6 petrol engines introduced with AWD as standard
5-speed manual or 5-speed automatic transmissions
Saloon body style only (estate launched 2004)
mainEngines: AJ-V6 AJ30 / WB - 3.0L petrol (231hp / 231 PS) - AWD
knownFor: Genuine Jaguar styling from Geoff Lawson's last design
AWD system with 60% rear / 40% front torque split

2.5 V6 became the most searched petrol variant for engine replacement

faq.h2: Jaguar X Type Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Jaguar X Type engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A replacement engine costs £600-£3,800 depending on condition and engine code. Used units start at £600-£1,800, rebuilt engines run £1,200-£2,800, and fully reconditioned units with full warranty cost £1,400-£3,200. Fitting adds £400-£800.
keyPoints: Used DLD-420 2.0D engines: £600-£1,200 with typical salvage mileage
Rebuilt AJ-V6 2.5L petrol: £1,400-£2,800 supply only
Reconditioned XB engine average: £1,412 (range £1,337-£1,533)
Full supply and fit package: engine price plus £400-£800 labour
Dealer fitted genuine Jaguar stripped engine: over £3,600 plus fitting
items.cta: Compare Jaguar X Type engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is the Jaguar X Type 2.0D diesel engine reliable? What fails?
items.answer: The 2.0D Duratorq is generally robust but has three well-documented failure points: injector seal oil dilution (DLD-420), EGR cooler clogging, and fuel pump contamination that can destroy injectors. Regular maintenance significantly extends life.
keyPoints: DLD-420 injector seal failure causes diesel to contaminate engine oil
Fuel pump disintegration contaminates all injectors via common rail system
2.0D EGR cooler clogging triggers limp mode at 60k-80k miles
DPF issues on post-2006 models used for short journeys
2.2D adds swirl flap failure in plastic inlet manifold
items.cta: Get quotes for reconditioned Jaguar X Type 2.0D diesel engines

items.question: What causes timing chain failure on the Jaguar X Type V6 petrol engines?
items.answer: Plastic timing chain tensioners lose hydraulic pressure when the engine is off, allowing chain slack. On cold start, the chain rattles until oil pressure builds. If ignored, tensioners fail completely, causing chain skip and piston-to-valve contact.
keyPoints: AJ-V6 2.5L and 3.0L use plastic tensioners that wear after 80k-120k miles
1cm of cold chain slack indicates tensioner may be at maximum extension
Silent running does NOT guarantee safety - slack visible only with valve cover off
Genuine Jaguar timing chain kit includes guides, tensioner and crank sprocket
Fits 2001-2008 X-Type 2.5L and 3.0L V6 petrol models
items.cta: Compare rebuilt AJ-V6 petrol engine prices with upgraded metal tensioners

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a high-mileage Jaguar X Type?
items.answer: For an X Type worth £1,500-£4,000, specialist-fit rebuilt engine at £1,800-£3,200 is often worthwhile if the body and transmission are sound. Dealer-priced replacement at £4,000+ rarely makes financial sense. Owner sentiment strongly favours saving well-maintained examples.
keyPoints: Well-maintained X Types regularly exceed 200,000 miles
Forum owners report spending more than car's value to keep X Types on road
Specialist rebuild costs 40-60% below main dealer prices
2.5 V6 with 191,000 miles still running strong on original chain
Used donor engine from breaker: £600-£1,000 plus fitting
items.cta: Find out if your Jaguar X Type qualifies for cost-effective engine replacement

items.question: Can I replace my Jaguar X Type engine with a Ford Mondeo engine?
items.answer: Short answer: No. While the Duratorq diesel and AJ-V6 petrol engines share architecture with Ford Mondeo units, X Type engines require Jaguar-specific ancillaries, ECU calibration, engine mounts, and in some cases different oil pans and sump configurations.
keyPoints: DLD-420 diesel shares block with Mondeo TDCi but has unique calibration
AJ-V6 petrol uses Jaguar-specific ECU mapping and ancillaries
AWD versions require different sump and oil pick-up configuration
Engine code matching (DLD-416, DLD-420, AJ-V6 XC) is critical
Always buy X Type-specific replacement - not generic Ford unit
items.cta: Compare Jaguar X Type engine codes and verify compatibility before ordering

items.question: What are the most common Jaguar X Type engine problems by engine code?
items.answer: DLD-420: injector seal oil dilution. DLD-416/420: EGR cooler failure. 214D2: swirl flap breakage. AJ-V6 XC/XJ: timing chain tensioner wear. AJ-V6 YB (2.1L): coil pack failure. Each has distinct failure patterns and repair costs.
keyPoints: DLD-420 - injector seal failure causing oil dilution
DLD-416/420 - EGR cooler and inlet manifold gasket failure
214D2/214D3 - swirl flap ingestion and turbo actuator failure
AJ-V6 XC/XJ - timing chain tensioner wear (80k-120k miles)
AJ-V6 YB (2.1L) - individual coil pack failures common
items.cta: Get quotes specific to your Jaguar X Type engine code

items.question: What is the life expectancy of a Jaguar X Type 2.5 V6 engine?
items.answer: With meticulous maintenance including 3,000-5,000 mile oil changes, the AJ-V6 2.5L regularly exceeds 200,000 miles. Timing chain tensioners are the main vulnerability. One forum owner reports 191,000 miles on original chain with no noise but visible slack.
keyPoints: Well-documented example at 191,000 miles running strongly
3,000-mile oil changes with quality oil recommended
Timing chain tensioner replacement at 80k-120k miles is preventative
Engine condition depends more on maintenance history than mileage
Many X Types scrapped due to repair costs, not engine failure
items.cta: Compare Jaguar X Type 2.5 V6 rebuilt engine prices with warranty

items.question: How do I know if my Jaguar X Type needs an engine replacement or just repair?
items.answer: Knocking or rumbling from bottom end = likely replacement. Cold start rattle only = timing chain tensioners (£1,200-£2,000 at specialist). Diesel oil smell on dipstick = injector seals (£250-£450). Coolant loss with no visible leak = possible head gasket or cracked crossover pipe.
keyPoints: Timing chain rattle (1-2 seconds cold start) = preventative chain replacement
Continuous rattle or loud bang = catastrophic failure - replacement needed
Diesel smell in oil = injector seal replacement if caught early
Overheating from coolant pipe crack = pipe replacement if caught immediately
Bottom end knock = replacement or rebuild - repair not viable
items.cta: Get a free assessment - compare Jaguar X Type repair vs replacement quotes

items.question: Are reconditioned Jaguar X Type engines as good as new?
items.answer: Quality reconditioned engines from specialist rebuilders often exceed factory specifications, using upgraded components like metal timing chain tensioners (replacing plastic originals) and improved injector seals. Every unit is bench tested before dispatch - unlike new crate engines which aren't run-in.
keyPoints: Upgraded metal timing chain tensioners address original design flaw
Reconditioned engines include new rings, bearings, seals throughout
Bench tested before shipping - factory engines are not run-in
12-month unlimited mileage warranty minimum with all units
Typical reconditioned XB engine cost: £1,400-£1,500 supply only
items.cta: Compare reconditioned Jaguar X Type engines with 12-month warranty

items.question: Can I get a Jaguar X Type engine with supply and fit included?
items.answer: Yes. EnginesMarket connects you with UK specialists offering full supply and fit packages. Fitting typically adds £400-£800 to the engine price, depending on variant (AWD takes longer than FWD) and your location. All work guaranteed by the fitting garage.
keyPoints: AWD X Types require more labour time than FWD variants
X400 chassis requires specific tooling for AJ-V6 timing alignment
Supply and fit removes need to find specialist willing to fit customer engine
Fitting labour typically 8-12 hours for diesel, 10-14 hours for AWD petrol
All work guaranteed by network-approved Jaguar specialists
items.cta: Find Jaguar X Type engine supply and fit deals near you
